note: File bridge + .design.mmd save/load. TWO artifacts. (1) A dev-only Vite plugin `vite-file-bridge.mjs` (root, sibling of vite-agent-bridge.mjs) exposing read/list/write endpoints under /novakai/designs, scoped to a new top-level `designs/` folder — DEV INFRA, not a src node, so not tracked here; registered in vite.config.ts's `command==='serve' && !process.env.CI` plugin array. Its pure guards are unit-tested in vite-file-bridge.test.mjs (mirroring vite-agent-bridge.test.mjs). SECURITY (trust boundary — arbitrary FS write from a browser; no laziness): the plugin MUST enforce, all as pure testable helpers — (a) loopback-only via the existing isLoopback pattern; (b) name charset allowlist `^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$` rejecting `/`, `\`, `..`, dots, absolute paths and empty — so the name can never carry a path; (c) containment via `path.resolve(designsDir, name + '.design.mmd')` then a prefix check against `designsDir + path.sep` (NOT bare startsWith on the dir — that admits a sibling `designs-evil`); (d) the `.design.mmd` extension appended SERVER-side, never trusted from the client; (e) method restriction: write only on POST, list/read only on GET. RESIDUAL, named and accepted (mirrors the agent bridge's posture): loopback authenticates the socket, not the origin, so a site the dev visits could CSRF a cross-origin POST to localhost — bounded here because the only effect is writing `.design.mmd` text into `designs/`, never executed. (2) src-side logic colocated in the EXISTING io module `files` (src/io/files.ts) so every new node's parent already exists in the base map: two pure serialize/parse functions (acceptance-bearing) plus three bridge-client fetch methods (verification=journey), consumed by a modified `filesPage` (src/ide/files.ts) render. INVARIANT 2 WIRING: filesPage reaches io/files only through ctx.hooks; and io/files must NOT import design-model — the UI-json source and sink cross a module boundary and are pinned to two hooks wired in main.ts: `ctx.hooks.getDesignDraft(): string` (returns the current design-tab draft json, i.e. the raw novakai.design.v1 value) and `ctx.hooks.restoreDesignDraft(json: string): void` (writes it back and refreshes the design tab). saveDesign uses mermaid.toMermaid() for the body and getDesignDraft() for the ui json; loadDesign feeds parseDesignFile(text).body to the existing loadMmdText path and .uiJson to restoreDesignDraft. FORMAT: the .design.mmd is the plain toMermaid() body (positions already round-trip via %% fm) plus ONE trailing `%% design-ui <uiJson>` envelope line. uiJson is JSON.stringify with no indent, so it is single-line by construction — that is exactly what makes the trailing-line strip unambiguous (toMermaid emits all its %% lines at the top; the body's last line is always a node/edge). DEGRADATION: the bridge is absent in build/CI (GitHub Pages), so listDesigns/loadDesign MUST catch a failed fetch and the Files tab MUST render an empty/disabled state rather than throw — journey verification covers dev only. A seed designs/example.design.mmd makes the folder real in the tree and pins the format. `.design.mmd` under designs/ is invisible to every gate (bundle/coverage/exports/edit-gate/roadmap:audit) — confirmed against the src-scoped selectors.

intent.problem: A design draft (diagram + UI state) has no single on-disk form; it lives only in two localStorage keys — the exact non-verifiable state novakai exists to kill.
intent.approach: A pure serializer: the toMermaid() body plus one trailing `%% design-ui <uiJson>` envelope line, so the whole draft round-trips as one .design.mmd text with no separate positions store (positions already live in %% fm). uiJson is single-line (JSON.stringify, no indent).
intent.rationale: One file, one serializer boundary (io/files, wrapping toMermaid — grammar stays in io/mermaid), deterministic bytes — the draft becomes a diffable, reviewable artifact instead of opaque browser storage.

fm.name: toDesignFile
fm.description: Serialize a design draft to .design.mmd text: the mermaid body, then a single trailing `%% design-ui <uiJson>` envelope line.

cases.name: wraps the mermaid body with the ui envelope
empty ui still emits the envelope marker

intent.problem: Loading a .design.mmd needs the mermaid body back (to render the diagram) and the ui json back (to restore the draft), from one scan.
intent.approach: Split off a single trailing `%% design-ui ...` line: return { body, uiJson }. No envelope present → { body: the full text, uiJson: '' }.
intent.rationale: The parse half of the round-trip contract as ONE function and one acceptance surface (auditor's merge of the earlier body/ui split); safe because the envelope is a single line by construction.

fm.name: parseDesignFile
fm.description: Parse .design.mmd text into { body, uiJson } by splitting off a single trailing `%% design-ui ...` envelope line; uiJson is '' when no envelope is present.

cases.name: no envelope returns full body and empty ui

intent.problem: The Files tab must show what design drafts exist on disk, and must not throw when the bridge is absent (prod/CI).
intent.approach: GET /novakai/designs from the dev file bridge, returning the list of .design.mmd base names; catch fetch failure and return [] so the tab degrades to an empty state.
intent.rationale: Read side of the bridge; the browser cannot list the repo without the dev-server plugin, and the shipped build has no plugin.

fm.name: listDesigns
fm.description: List saved design draft names by GETting /novakai/designs; returns [] if the dev file bridge is absent.

intent.problem: A design draft must persist to the repo as a real file, not localStorage.
intent.approach: Serialize via toDesignFile(mermaid.toMermaid(), ctx.hooks.getDesignDraft()) and POST /novakai/designs/write {name, text} to the dev bridge.
intent.rationale: Write side; turns the in-memory draft into a committed, reviewable designs/<name>.design.mmd. UI json comes through ctx.hooks, never a design-model import (invariant 2).

fm.name: saveDesign
fm.description: Persist the current draft as designs/<name>.design.mmd via POST to the dev file bridge, using toMermaid() and ctx.hooks.getDesignDraft().

intent.problem: A saved design draft must reopen into the editor and restore the UI draft state.
intent.approach: GET the .design.mmd text (catch failure → no-op), parseDesignFile(text) → feed .body to the existing loadMmdText renderer and .uiJson to ctx.hooks.restoreDesignDraft.
intent.rationale: Completes the bridge round-trip; reuses loadMmdText rather than a second parser, and restores the draft through the hook seam (invariant 2).

fm.name: loadDesign
fm.description: Load designs/<name>.design.mmd via the dev bridge and restore both the diagram (loadMmdText) and the UI draft (ctx.hooks.restoreDesignDraft); no-op if the bridge is absent.

intent.problem: The Files tab is an empty stub; it must surface the designs/ folder — list, open, and save the current draft — and degrade cleanly when the bridge is absent.
intent.approach: Replace the empty-state render with a designs list (ctx.hooks.listDesigns), an open action per row (ctx.hooks.loadDesign), and a save control (ctx.hooks.saveDesign); an empty list renders the existing empty state. The tab reaches io/files only through ctx.hooks, never by import.
intent.rationale: Makes the bridge usable and demonstrable end to end while honouring invariant 2 and staying dev/prod-safe.

fm.name: initFilesPage
fm.description: Files tab: lists designs/*.design.mmd via the dev file bridge, opens a draft into the editor, and saves the current draft — replacing the K7 empty-state stub, empty/disabled when the bridge is absent.
